headline: SpaceX Is in the Nasdaq-100, Now Brace for Volatility
summary: SpaceX  trading on Tuesday won’t have much to do with space or AI fundamentals.  Elon Musk’s AI and space company is now in the Nasdaq-100, one of the best-known large-cap growth indexes that tracks the largest nonfinancial companies listed on Nasdaq.  Being fast-tracked into the index reflects just how important privately held frontier AI companies, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX before its June IPO, have become to the entire stock market.

content.title: SpaceX Is in the Nasdaq-100, Now Brace for Volatility
content.summary: SpaceX  trading on Tuesday won’t have much to do with space or AI fundamentals.  Elon Musk’s AI and space company is now in the Nasdaq-100, one of the best-known large-cap growth indexes that tracks the largest nonfinancial companies listed on Nasdaq.  Being fast-tracked into the index reflects just how important privately held frontier AI companies, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX before its June IPO, have become to the entire stock market.

headline: Is Ford Motor (F) Fairly Valued After Its Micron Chip Supply Deal?
summary: Ford Motor (F) is back in focus after announcing a long term supply agreement with Micron Technology to secure memory and storage chips for its next generation vehicles, a key input for increasingly software centric cars. See our latest analysis for Ford Motor. At a share price of US$13.83, Ford Motor has seen a 20.05% 90 day share price return and a 25.21% 1 year total shareholder return, suggesting recent momentum after a softer 30 day period. If the Ford chip agreement has you thinking...

headline: Micron Locks in Big Customers But Memory-Chip Fears Drag on Stock
summary: Micron Technology  was falling early Tuesday after stock moves in South Korea spooked investors in the memory-chip sector.  Micron shares were down 6.0% at $925.61 in premarket trading.  Fellow memory-chip company  SK Hynix  fell 6.1%, as it prepares to list American depositary receipts on the Nasdaq on Friday, raising $28 billion.

content.title: Micron Locks in Big Customers But Memory-Chip Fears Drag on Stock
content.summary: Micron Technology  was falling early Tuesday after stock moves in South Korea spooked investors in the memory-chip sector.  Micron shares were down 6.0% at $925.61 in premarket trading.  Fellow memory-chip company  SK Hynix  fell 6.1%, as it prepares to list American depositary receipts on the Nasdaq on Friday, raising $28 billion.

headline: Sandisk Stock Was Red Hot but Get Set for a Cold Snap
summary: Sandisk  has been one of the hottest U.S. tech stocks over the past year but it’s starting to cool off. headline: Tech Stocks Drop as Investors Lock in Profit Amid AI Jitters
summary: Tech looked set to struggle on Monday as investors dumped chip makers and a host of other stocks that have benefited from the AI boom. Futures tracking the Nasdaq 100 were 0.9% lower, dragged down by heavyweights including memory-chip maker Micron and semiconductor manufacturer Intel.

Futures tracking the Nasdaq 100 were 0.9% lower, dragged down by heavyweights including memory-chip maker Micron and semiconductor manufacturer Intel.
